title: LOW: client_secret stored plaintext in database
description: In src/api.rs line 349, the client_secret is stored as-is in oauth_apps. While this is standard practice for OAuth2 (the spec allows it, and Mastodon does the same), it means a database leak exposes all client secrets. The constant-time comparison on line 777 protects against timing attacks on the wire but doesn't help if the DB is compromised. Consider hashing client_secret with SHA-256 at storage time (same approach used for access tokens). This is a defense-in-depth improvement, not a protocol violation.
